sentence: Subjective:
The patient, an 82-year-old male, presents for hospital follow-up after an acute on chronic congestive heart failure exacerbation.
He reports feeling well but notes that he had been trying to watch his diet, but experienced swelling in his ankles and shortness of breath.
He was hospitalized for 2 days and received treatment, including a medication that caused him to urinate frequently and a water pill given through his IV.
He reports feeling much better now.
He has been taking his medications as instructed since returning home and has been monitoring his blood pressure with a cuff he purchased.
He denies shortness of breath, chest pain, or problems sleeping.
Past medical history:
The patient has a significant past medical history of hypertension and stage three chronic kidney disease.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications:
I prescribed bumex 2 milligrams daily for congestive heart failure; cozaar 100 milligrams daily for hypertension; and norvasc 5 milligrams once daily for hypertension.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure today is 128/72, heart rate is 79, respiratory rate is 26, and oxygen saturation is 99%.
Physical exam findings: The patient's neck exam is normal with no jugular venous distention.
The heart exam reveals a stable 2/6 systolic ejection murmur.
The lungs are clear bilaterally.
The patient's lower extremities show a trace of edema.
Test Results:
The echocardiogram shows a preserved EF of 55%, abnormal diastolic filling, and mild to moderate mitral regurgitation.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has congestive heart failure caused by dietary indiscretion and uncontrolled hypertension, stage three chronic kidney disease, and hypertension.
Problem list: Congestive heart failure; chronic kidney disease; hypertension; suspected secondary causes of hypertension; kidney disease.
Plan:
I want the patient to continue on bumex 2 milligrams daily for congestive heart failure and to continue to watch his diet and avoid salty foods.
I also want him to weigh himself daily and call me if he gains 3 pounds in 2 days.
I will refer him to a nutritionist for education on healthy eating.
I will continue him on cozaar 100 milligrams daily and norvasc 5 milligrams once daily for hypertension.
I will order a renal artery ultrasound to rule out secondary causes of hypertension.
I will also obtain additional labs to monitor his kidney disease.
Follow-up:
The patient will be seen again in 3 months.
